skill.name: agent-orchestration-improve-agent
skill.description: Systematic improvement of existing agents through performance analysis, prompt engineering, and continuous iteration.
skill.summary: Improve existing agents with metrics, prompt changes, tests, rollout controls, and review cycles.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found no executable code or malicious instruction. The command hits are Markdown fences and workflow examples in SKILL.md, while the other hits are ordinary prose in the same file. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential access, or network behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Agent quality can decline when teams change prompts without shared metrics. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for measuring, improving, testing, and rolling out agent changes.
actual_capabilities: Defines baseline metrics for task success, corrections, latency, tool use, and token use.
Guides failure mode analysis for prompt quality, context loss, tool misuse, and format errors.
Suggests prompt improvements using examples, role refinement, self-checks, and output templates.
Outlines A/B testing with representative tasks, blind review, and statistical significance checks.
Provides staged rollout and rollback criteria for safer production changes.
limitations: Requires existing agent metrics, user feedback, or representative test cases.
Does not create a new agent architecture from scratch.
Does not include executable test tooling or automated dashboards.
Human review is still needed for subjective quality and safety judgments.

use_cases.title: Improve a support agent
use_cases.description: Analyze failed support sessions, add better examples, and validate improved answers before rollout.

use_cases.title: Tune a coding assistant
use_cases.description: Compare prompt versions on common coding tasks, tool use, latency, and regression cases.

use_cases.title: Create an evaluation cadence
use_cases.description: Set weekly and monthly review loops for metrics, user feedback, and future optimization work.

prompt_templates.title: Baseline review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis of an existing agent
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my existing agent. Identify baseline metrics I should collect from recent tasks and explain how to organize them.

prompt_templates.title: Failure mode analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to classify these agent failures. Group them by root cause, likely prompt issue, and recommended fix priority.

prompt_templates.title: Prompt improvement plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design improvements for this agent prompt. Include better role definition, examples, self-checks, and measurable success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: A/B rollout plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an A/B test and staged rollout plan for an improved agent. Include sample size, metrics, rollback triggers, and post-launch review.

output_examples.input: Review an agent that often gives incomplete answers.
output_examples.output: A baseline plan covering completion rate, correction frequency, missed requirements, representative examples, and a prioritized list of likely prompt changes.

output_examples.input: Compare two versions of a coding assistant prompt.
output_examples.output: An evaluation plan with shared task sets, success metrics, blind review criteria, token and latency tracking, and a rollout recommendation.

output_examples.input: Prepare a safer rollout for a prompt update.
output_examples.output: A staged deployment plan with alpha, beta, canary, monitoring, rollback triggers, and a 30-day review process.

best_practices: Start with representative failures and successful sessions before changing prompts.
Measure new behavior against the baseline with the same tasks and scoring rubric.
Document rollback triggers before sending an improved agent to production users.
anti_patterns: Do not optimize from a few memorable failures without checking broader data.
Do not ship prompt changes without regression tests for prior successful behavior.
Do not treat faster responses as better when correctness or safety declines.

faq.question: Does this skill improve agents automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides analysis, prompt changes, testing, rollout, and review. A user still applies and validates the changes.

faq.question: What data should I bring?
faq.answer: Bring recent tasks, failed outputs, user corrections, tool traces, latency, token use, and any satisfaction signals.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is written for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when improving existing agent behavior.

faq.question: Is it useful for brand-new agents?
faq.answer: It is not the best fit for a new agent. It assumes an existing agent with behavior to measure.

faq.question: Does it include runnable evaluation software?
faq.answer: No. It describes evaluation structure and metrics, but it does not ship scripts or a testing framework.

faq.question: How should teams use the results?
faq.answer: Teams should document the baseline, test changes against shared examples, roll out gradually, and review metrics after release.
